name: Eggplant Pilaf in Olive Oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the stem off the eggplant.
2. Using a vegetable peeler, peel lengthwise to remove a strip of skin; leave the next strip on. Continue peeling in this striped fashion.
3. Cube the eggplant into 1-inch cubes. Sprinkle generously with salt and set aside for 3 hours.
4. Rinse the eggplant well and drain as much as possible without tearing it.
5. Soak the rice in hot water mixed with 2 teaspoons of salt until the water turns cool. Drain well and set aside.
6. Heat olive oil in a heavy pan and cook the eggplant cubes, stirring frequently, until tender and golden brown on all sides. Drain to remove excess oil.
7. Add onions and pine nuts to the same pan. Sauté for 15 to 20 minutes.
8. Stir in the rice and cook, stirring frequently, for 8 to 10 minutes. Then stir in the eggplant and remaining ingredients except dill.
9. Add 3 cups of hot water, bring to a boil, cover, and cook over medium heat for 5 minutes. Reduce heat to low and cook until all the water has been absorbed.
10. Stir in the fresh dill.
11. Ensure that the skillet lid has a very good seal. Cover the skillet with a clean towel and put the lid on top of that. Set the pan on a heat diffuser over the lowest possible heat and leave it for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
12. Serve cold.
